summary: Clear audience tape with solid sound but a hurried performance; Jerry shines on Eyes of the World while Help‑Slip feels rushed.
review: The recording captures a fairly clear PA mix that balances Brent’s keyboards better than many soundboard tapes, though Jerry is low in the mix. Musically the show runs at breakneck speed – the Help > Slip sequence feels overly rushed and Bob Weir struggles with lyrics on Saturday Night, but the band stretches out nicely on Eyes of the World with a stellar Jerry solo.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is moderate (rushed tempo, mixed performance) while recording quality is good/clear; weighted average yields ~3.3 stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Great overall sound with a clear PA mix; emphasizes Brent Mydland on keyboards and down‑mixes Jerry, but remains listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set races at a high tempo, making Help‑Slip feel rushed and vocals tight, but eases into a relaxed jam on Eyes of the World that showcases Jerry's improvisational skill.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Stellar solo on Eyes of the World; however his guitar is low in the PA mix.
band_member_comments.Bob: Struggles with lyrics on Saturday Night, sounding rushed.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland is prominent in the mix, providing a more balanced keyboard presence than typical SBDs.
